statement.markdown: Given a Docker container configuration object, write a function to fix exposed port mappings. If the `EXPOSE` instruction is specified multiple times, combine them into a single list of ports. If no `EXPOSE` instruction is specified, use the default port 80. The function should return the fixed `EXPOSE` instruction as a string.
statement.inputFormat: A JSON object containing the container configuration, e.g. `{ 'EXPOSE': ['8080', '8081'] }`
statement.outputFormat: The fixed `EXPOSE` instruction as a string, e.g. `'8080 8081'`
statement.notes: The function should handle cases where the `EXPOSE` instruction is specified multiple times, as well as cases where it is not specified at all.

constraints.description: Number of ports in the `EXPOSE` instruction

examples.explanation: The `EXPOSE` instruction is specified multiple times, so the function should combine them into a single list of ports.
The `EXPOSE` instruction is specified once, so the function should return it as is.
The `EXPOSE` instruction is not specified, so the function should use the default port 80.

hints.text: Check if the `EXPOSE` instruction is specified multiple times and combine the ports if so.

editorial.title: Fixing Docker Exposed Port Mappings
editorial.intuition: The function should handle cases where the `EXPOSE` instruction is specified multiple times, as well as cases where it is not specified at all.
editorial.bruteForce: Check if the `EXPOSE` instruction is specified multiple times and combine the ports if so. If not specified, use the default port 80.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use the `join` method to combine the ports into a single string if the `EXPOSE` instruction is specified multiple times. If not specified, use the default port 80.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The function should return the correct `EXPOSE` instruction as a string for all test cases.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to handle cases where the `EXPOSE` instruction is not specified.
